title: DA, DR hike: Dearness allowance for pensioners — Dearness Relief calculation, eligibility, explained
summary: Dearness Allowance: A dedicated percentage of pensioners monthly pension payout is aimed at mitigating inflation. However, unlike DA in salary for central government employees, the component is known as Dearness Relief or DR.

title: Newly married couples: 8 health insurance mistakes to avoid
summary: Newly married couples should review health insurance plans, assess coverage needs and plan for maternity benefits. This guide highlights common mistakes, including relying only on employer cover and overlooking parents’ healthcare needs, to help couples make smarter financial decisions.

title: Retired PSU employee faces ₹2 lakh tax demand over Form 16 omission; ITAT Pune grants relief
summary: A retired PSU employee successfully challenged a ₹2 lakh tax demand after ITAT Pune ruled that a leave encashment exemption cannot be denied because it was omitted from Form 16. The Tribunal held that statutory tax benefits are determined by law and not by how the employer reports them.

title: ITR Filing 2026: CBDT upgrades income tax portal before July 31 deadline, Key changes explained
summary: The Finance Ministry has told Parliament that the Income Tax Department has strengthened the e-filing portal to handle peak traffic during the return filing season, with infrastructure capable of processing up to 1 crore ITRs a day.

title: Can cooperative insurance become India’s next financial inclusion story?
summary: The proposed national cooperative life insurer is about more than adding another player—it could shift India's insurance journey from expanding access to building financial resilience.

title: Can the government impose taxes without a law? What Article 265 says
summary: Article 265 of the Indian Constitution states that no tax can be levied or collected except by the authority of law. In simple terms, every tax must be backed by a valid law passed by Parliament or a state legislature, preventing arbitrary taxation by the government.

title: Cable and DTH charges to rise 4-6 times? PIB Fact Check reveals the truth
summary: PIB Fact Check has dismissed viral claims that the Centre's draft Telecom Rules, 2026 will shut down TVs or raise cable and DTH charges by four to six times. It clarified that the draft rules contain no fee hike provision and are currently open for public consultation.

title: SGB 2019-20 Series VIII redemption opens today: Check payout, eligibility and tax rules
summary: RBI has opened premature redemption for SGB 2019-20 Series VIII at ₹14,170 per unit from July 21. Before opting for an early exit, investors should understand the eligibility conditions, redemption process and the capital gains tax rules under the Income-tax Act, 2025.

title: GIFT City investments and Schedule FA reporting: why taxpayers are seeking greater clarity
summary: As GIFT City opens global investment opportunities for Indian residents, a key tax question remains unresolved: Do investments in IFSC funds qualify as foreign assets for Schedule FA reporting?

title: EPS-95 pension hike: Will Centre raise minimum pension from ₹1,000 to ₹7,500?
summary: EPS-95 pensioners’ demand for a hike in the minimum pension from ₹1,000 to ₹7,500 per month remains unresolved. The Minister of State for Labour and Employment acknowledged that, though several stakeholders have sought a pension hike, no decision has been made on the matter yet.&nbsp;

title: Adani Total Gas Q1 Results: Profit falls 14% YoY to Rs 142 crore, revenue jumps 27%
summary: Adani Total Gas Q1 Results: Adani Total Gas reported 14% year-on-year (YoY) decline in its consolidated net profit at Rs 142 crore in the first quarter. The same stood at Rs 165 crore in the same quarter of last year. Revenue from operations rose 27% YoY to Rs 1,907 crore.

title: Global Market: Euro zone banks tighten credit standards amid geopolitical risks, ECB survey shows
summary: Euro zone banks tightened lending standards in the second quarter as geopolitical uncertainty and economic risks prompted greater caution, according to the ECB's latest Bank Lending Survey. summary: TVS Motor Company's net profit surged sixty-seven percent year-on-year to one thousand nineteen crore rupees. title: Global Market: London Stock Exchange to roll out overnight trading exchange LSE 24 in 2027
summary: The new exchange will operate separately from the LSE's main market and will initially offer trading in exchange-traded products (ETPs), including funds that track the UK and U.S. stock markets, according to Reuters.

title: Dollar wavers as markets grapple with Gulf tensions
summary: The US dollar hovered near a one-week high as investors weighed escalating Middle East tensions against hopes for a ceasefire. title: Gold rises 1% as hopes for US-Iran diplomacy pause oil rally
summary: Gold prices gained over 1% as investors tracked diplomatic efforts to ease the US-Iran conflict and assessed the impact on inflation and the Federal Reserve’s rate outlook. summary: Euro zone government bond yields are rising as oil prices remain elevated. This situation threatens inflation outlook and supports tighter European Central Bank policy. title: Julius Baer net new money beats expectations, but de-risking clouds outlook
summary: Julius Baer reported net new money inflows of 5.7 billion Swiss francs in the first half of 2026, beating expectations despite a slow start to the year.
